rural-specialist.content: Command(update={'files': {}, 'reexamine_results': [], 'usage': [], 'messages': [ToolMessage(content='{"candidates":["Colombia","Brazil","Mexico"],"evidence":["Landscape & Soil: The very flat, dry savanna with distinctly reddish/orange soil is a perfect match for the \'Los Llanos\' region in Colombia (particularly the departments of Meta and Casanare) and the \'Cerrado\' in Brazil.","Road Markings: Solid double yellow center lines and white outer edge lines are the standard configuration for major rural roads in both Colombia and Brazil.","Vegetation: The presence of palms in the distance (likely Mauritia flexuosa) and sparse tall trees is characteristic of the South American tropical savanna biomes.","Infrastructure: High-voltage metal lattice transmission towers are ubiquitous in the flat plains of Colombia and Brazil to bridge large distances between power sources and cities.","Vehicle Clue: The white utility vehicle with a roof rack/antenna is a well-known indicator for Colombia in regional image analysis, specifically the \'Colombia antenna\' meta-clue."],"contradictions":["Mexico features diverse landscapes, but the specific combination of deep red soil and extremely flat tropical savanna is less common than in the Llanos or Cerrado regions."],"confidence":90}', name='task', tool_call_id='0u8snljl')]})
